en.commentary: The 2026 home security camera market has hit a genuine turning point: local AI processing has matured enough to kill the case for mandatory cloud subscriptions. Four out of five cameras launched in Q1 2026 ship with zero monthly fees, and several deliver AI detection accuracy that matches or beats subscription-dependent rivals from two years ago. The Reolink Argus 4 Pro is my pick for the top spot. The 180° dual-lens design eliminates every dead angle a single-lens camera leaves, and ColorX technology delivers genuine full-color footage in near-total darkness without blasting floodlight LEDs across the yard — a detail that matters at 2am when you want to identify a face, not just detect motion. Wi-Fi 6 keeps the 4K stream stable in a crowded home network, and the included solar panel means battery anxiety is simply off the table. At around $220 with no ongoing fees, the value calculation is decisive. Arlo's Pro 5S earns the second spot on the strength of its AI accuracy — 94% motion detection precision in independent testing is the number no competitor has matched this year. The 8-month battery life is also best-in-class for a camera with this level of detection capability. The mandatory Arlo Secure subscription for cloud recording is a real cost to factor in, but the hardware performance justifies the premium if AI precision is your first priority. eufy's SoloCam S340 takes third for anyone who wants 360° pan-tilt coverage with zero subscription compromise. The 3K resolution is sharp enough, the HomeBase provides solid local storage, and the solar charging works reliably in most climates. Wyze Cam v4 closes the list at rank 8, but that ranking undersells what it achieves: 2.5K resolution, AI detection, Wi-Fi 6, and color night vision for $36 is a hardware achievement that would have been impossible two years ago. If you are outfitting a property with multiple cameras on a tight budget, the v4 is the obvious answer.

highlights.title: Reolink Argus 4 Pro: the no-subscription benchmark for 2026
highlights.body: The 180° dual-lens configuration is the key differentiator — it covers a driveway or backyard with one camera where competitors need two. ColorX night vision keeps full color in conditions down to 0.0001 lux, which means recognizable footage when it actually matters. Wi-Fi 6 dual-band connectivity holds a stable 4K stream even in dense multi-device home networks. All of this comes with solar power and no monthly fee, at a price point that puts it squarely against cameras that require ongoing subscriptions to match the feature set.

highlights.title: Arlo Pro 5S: the right choice when AI accuracy is non-negotiable
highlights.body: Independent testing by SafeHome.org logged a 94% motion detection accuracy rate for the Pro 5S — the highest in its class this year. The 2K HDR sensor delivers genuinely useful detail at 12x zoom, and the 8-month battery life is exceptional for a camera running this level of processing. The Arlo Secure subscription starts at $7.99 per month for cloud recording and full AI features, which adds roughly $100 per year per camera. Factor that in before buying, but if you are installing cameras at entry points where false alarms and missed events carry real consequences, the accuracy premium is worth it.

highlights.title: 2026's defining trend: local AI makes subscriptions optional
highlights.body: The structural shift in the 2026 market is that on-device AI processing has finally reached the point where cloud dependency is a feature, not a requirement. Reolink, eufy, aosu, Blink, and Wyze all ship cameras that run person detection, vehicle detection, and package detection entirely on-device or on a local hub. This means the subscription-vs-no-subscription decision is now a question of whether you want cloud backup and remote access, not whether you want smart detection at all. For most home users, local storage with a good microSD card or a NAS handles backup without recurring cost.

highlights.title: aosu SolarCam T2 Ultra: the technology leader that costs accordingly
highlights.body: The T2 Ultra's F/1.0 aperture and 1/1.8" starlight sensor combination represents the highest-spec night vision hardware in this roundup. TrueColor 4K footage in total darkness, without any supplemental lighting, is a genuine technical achievement. The triple detection system combining PIR heat sensing, wave radar, and on-device AI cuts false alarm rates below 10% in aosu's own testing. The system kit price starts at $700 for two cameras, which prices it out of the mainstream. For commercial installations, vacation properties, or anyone who treats physical security as a serious investment, the hardware justifies the cost.

highlights.title: Wyze Cam v4: what $36 buys in 2026
highlights.body: The v4's specification sheet reads like a mid-range camera from 2023: 2.5K QHD resolution, AI motion tracking, color night vision with dual spotlights, Wi-Fi 6 support, IP65 weatherproofing, and microSD recording up to 256GB. Wyze achieves this at $36 by running detection on-device and keeping the app ecosystem funded by optional CamPlus subscriptions. The wired-only design limits placement, but for a covered porch, garage interior, or driveway entry point with a nearby outlet, the v4 delivers monitoring capability that would have required a $150 camera in 2022.

zh-tw.commentary:
2026 年家用監控攝影機市場出現了一個真正的轉折點：本地 AI 運算的成熟度已經高到讓雲端強制訂閱失去存在理由。Q1 發表的新品裡，五台有四台主打零月費，而且好幾台的 AI 偵測準確率已經超越兩年前還得靠訂閱才能開啟的對手。

我把 Reolink Argus 4 Pro 排第一，理由很直接。180° 雙鏡頭設計把單鏡頭攝影機的死角問題直接解掉，一台抵兩台的覆蓋範圍，搭配 ColorX 技術在幾乎全黑的環境下輸出真彩色畫面，凌晨兩點發現可疑動靜你看到的是臉孔，不只是一個剪影。Wi-Fi 6 讓 4K 串流在多設備家庭網路裡保持穩定，附贈的太陽能板讓電量焦慮直接消失。大約 220 美元，零月費，CP 值計算根本不用猶豫。

Arlo Pro 5S 拿第二，靠的是 AI 準確率。獨立測試 94% 的移動偵測精確率是今年市場上沒有對手能複製的數字，8 個月電池壽命在這個效能等級裡也是最長的。Arlo Secure 訂閱費每月 7.99 美元起，一年下來大概多花一千元，買之前要算進去。但如果你裝的是主要出入口、不能漏報也不能誤報，這個準確率溢價是值得的。

eufy SoloCam S340 第三，給想要 360° 全向覆蓋又不想付月費的人。3K 畫質夠清楚，HomeBase 本地儲存夠可靠，太陽能在台灣氣候下充電效果穩定。

Wyze Cam v4 排第八，但這個名次並不代表它差。2.5K、AI 偵測、Wi-Fi 6、彩色夜視，全部塞進一台不到 1,200 元台幣的攝影機，這是 2022 年不可能發生的事。多點佈局的話，v4 是最省錢的方案。

highlights.title: Reolink Argus 4 Pro：2026 免訂閱攝影機的標竿
highlights.body: 180° 雙鏡頭是這台機器最關鍵的差異化設計，一台抵兩台，車道或後院的覆蓋範圍直接完整。ColorX 夜視在 0.0001 lux 的極暗環境仍能輸出全彩畫面，不需要打開 LED 泛光燈就能辨識人臉。Wi-Fi 6 雙頻連線在多設備的家庭網路裡維持 4K 串流穩定，附贈太陽能板加上零月費，220 美元的整體費用是今年這個功能等級裡最低的。

highlights.title: Arlo Pro 5S：AI 偵測準確率要求高就選這台
highlights.body: SafeHome.org 獨立測試記錄到 94% 的移動偵測準確率，這是今年市場上最高的數字。2K HDR 感光元件搭配 12 倍光學變焦，在需要辨識細節的場景下提供真正有用的畫質。8 個月電池壽命在這個效能等級的無線攝影機中是頂端。Arlo Secure 每月 7.99 美元起的訂閱費是真實的持有成本，換算一台相機一年額外支出約台幣 3,000 元，買前要納入計算。

highlights.title: 2026 年最重要的市場轉變：本地 AI 讓訂閱變成可選項
highlights.body: 這一代攝影機的結構性改變是，裝置端 AI 運算已經成熟到讓雲端依賴變成功能選項而非必要條件。Reolink、eufy、aosu、Blink、Wyze 的新品都能在本機或本地 Hub 上完成人形、車輛、包裹偵測，不需要連上雲端。這代表 2026 年選攝影機的問題不再是「要不要訂閱才能用 AI 偵測」，而是「你需不需要雲端備份和遠端存取」。對大多數家庭用戶來說，microSD 卡加上良好的本地備份方案，就能解決絕大部分需求。

highlights.title: aosu SolarCam T2 Ultra：技術最強，售價也最高
highlights.body: T2 Ultra 的 F/1.0 光圈加上 1/1.8" 星光感光元件，是這次評比裡規格最高的夜視硬體組合。TrueColor 4K 全彩夜視不需要任何補光就能達成，是真實的技術優勢。PIR 熱感應、波動雷達、裝置端 AI 三重偵測系統的結合，把誤報率壓到 10% 以下。兩台套裝起價 700 美元，在台灣換算大約 2 萬 2，進入門檻高。商業用途、度假別墅或者對安防當作認真投資的用戶，這個硬體規格值回票價。

highlights.title: Wyze Cam v4：36 美元在 2026 年能買到什麼
highlights.body: v4 的規格表看起來像 2023 年的中階機：2.5K QHD、AI 移動追蹤、雙 LED 彩色夜視、Wi-Fi 6、IP65 防水、最高 256GB microSD 錄影。Wyze 靠著裝置端偵測加上選配的 CamPlus 訂閱維持成本結構，讓這台機器可以賣到 36 美元。有線連接限制了安裝位置，但對於有插座的車道入口、車庫或遮雨棚，v4 提供的監控能力在 2022 年需要花四倍預算才能實現。

Best Outdoor Security Cameras of 2026

en.commentary:
Two strong additions to the list this week — the Reolink TrackFlex Floodlight WiFi and Baseus Security X1 Pro — expand the category's coverage into wired floodlight and AI dual-tracking solar camera territory that the existing eight did not address. Both carry zero subscription fees.

The core rankings hold steady. Reolink Argus 4 Pro continues to lead as the best no-subscription camera for most homes: 4K dual-lens 180-degree coverage, ColorX night vision at 0.0001 lux, Wi-Fi 6, solar power, and $220 with no ongoing fees. Arlo Pro 5S at rank 2 remains the AI detection precision leader at 94% in independent testing. For entry-point installations where false negatives carry real consequences, that accuracy margin over on-device competitors is worth the Arlo Secure subscription cost.

The Reolink TrackFlex Floodlight WiFi enters at rank 9 with an 8.3 overall score that understates its hardware quality. The 4K dual-lens configuration with 360° PTZ, 3000-lumen floodlights, and AI auto-tracking that locks onto a target and zooms in with the telephoto lens is a technically superior package. The limitation is that it requires hardwired AC installation, which places it outside the solar and battery camera segment where most of this list competes. For homeowners who can wire it to a junction box, this camera produces the best combination of resolution, floodlight output, and active tracking in the no-subscription category.

The Baseus Security X1 Pro at rank 10 enters the list as the only dual-target-tracking solar camera available. Its 7800mAh battery plus a 3W solar panel claims 150-day standby life, and Baseus's data shows 20 minutes of direct sunlight delivers 24 hours of runtime. The 300° pan range with dual 3K cameras covers a wider horizontal area than any battery-powered competitor here. The brand has a shorter reliability track record than Reolink, eufy, or Arlo, which is the reason for the conservative initial rank.

highlights.title: Reolink Argus 4 Pro's 180-degree dual-lens design eliminates the blind-spot problem
highlights.body: Single-lens cameras require careful placement to cover key areas without leaving gaps. The Argus 4 Pro's dual-lens 180-degree field of view eliminates that constraint — one camera covers what two conventional units would need to cover. ColorX night vision produces full-color footage at 0.0001 lux without supplemental lighting, which means identifiable footage at 2am without illuminating the yard. Wi-Fi 6 dual-band connectivity holds a stable 4K stream in homes with 30+ connected devices. At around $220 with no monthly fee, the value calculation against subscription-based alternatives is decisive.

highlights.title: Reolink TrackFlex Floodlight WiFi: the best wired security camera with no subscription in 2026
highlights.body: The TrackFlex Floodlight's dual-lens 360° PTZ design plus 3000-lumen floodlights and AI auto-tracking creates a qualitatively different security experience from battery or solar cameras. When motion is detected, the telephoto lens locks onto the target and follows it actively while the wide-angle lens maintains the full scene. The AI video search feature indexes footage locally — no cloud upload needed — so finding a specific event or face from a week's recordings takes seconds. At $260 hardwired with no subscription, it delivers features that competing smart floodlight cameras charge ongoing fees to access.

highlights.title: Baseus Security X1 Pro introduces AI dual-target tracking to the solar camera category
highlights.body: Most solar security cameras track one subject at a time. The Baseus X1 Pro's dual-camera system tracks two moving targets simultaneously, maintaining separate zoom locks for each. At $170 on sale with 300° horizontal pan, 512GB local storage, and a 150-day battery backed by solar charging, it sets a new benchmark for what a no-subscription solar camera can do. The brand's shorter market history relative to Reolink or eufy warrants a conservative initial ranking — track record takes time to establish — but the hardware specification is genuinely competitive.

highlights.title: 2026 security camera market: local AI has ended the subscription-or-nothing era
highlights.body: Three years ago, smart detection — person, vehicle, package differentiation — required a cloud subscription on almost every camera. In 2026, on-device neural processing handles these detection tasks at accuracy levels that would have required cloud compute in 2023. Every no-subscription camera in this updated list of ten runs full AI detection locally. The practical consequence is that the subscription decision is now about cloud backup and remote access, not intelligence. For most home security needs, a microSD card or local NAS with no monthly fee provides equivalent protection.

highlights.title: Wyze Cam v4 is the clearest demonstration of what 2026's silicon makes possible at $36
highlights.body: The v4's specification sheet would have described a $150 camera in 2022: 2.5K QHD resolution, AI motion tracking with person and vehicle differentiation, color night vision with dual spotlights, Wi-Fi 6, IP65 weatherproofing, and microSD recording up to 256GB. Wyze achieves this at $36 by running all detection on-device using dedicated AI silicon and monetizing through optional CamPlus subscriptions. For a covered porch, driveway, or garage with a nearby outlet, the v4 delivers monitoring capability that carries genuine insurance and security value.

zh-tw.commentary:
這週新增兩台攝影機進入排行：Reolink TrackFlex Floodlight WiFi 和 Baseus Security X1 Pro。前者填補了有線 AC 泛光燈攝影機的空缺，後者是 AI 雙目標追蹤太陽能機的首個代表，兩台都是零月費。

核心排名維持穩定。Reolink Argus 4 Pro 繼續領先，理由沒有改變：4K 雙鏡頭 180° 無死角、ColorX 夜視不需要開燈就能在極暗環境輸出全彩畫面、Wi-Fi 6、太陽能，220 美元零月費。Arlo Pro 5S 排第二，94% 移動偵測準確率的獨立測試成績在今年市場上仍然沒有對手，對裝在主要出入口、不能漏報的場景，Arlo Secure 訂閱費是值得的溢價。

Reolink TrackFlex Floodlight WiFi 以 8.3 分進入第九名，這個名次其實低估了它的硬體水準。4K 雙鏡頭 360° PTZ 旋轉、3000 流明泛光燈、AI 自動追蹤鎖定目標並切換到望遠鏡頭跟拍，是這個榜單裡免訂閱陣營中技術規格最完整的有線安防攝影機。限制是需要接 AC 有線電源安裝，排除了這份清單大多數競品所在的太陽能和電池攝影機使用場景。對於能夠走明線或暗管的用戶，這台機器提供了免訂閱市場裡解析度、照明輸出和主動追蹤的最強組合。

Baseus Security X1 Pro 以第十名初次進入排行，是目前唯一能同時追蹤兩個目標的太陽能攝影機。7800mAh 電池加 3W 太陽能板，Baseus 聲稱 20 分鐘陽光可供 24 小時使用，待機壽命 150 天。300° 水平旋轉加上雙 3K 鏡頭，覆蓋範圍超過這份清單任何電池攝影機。排名保守是因為品牌上市時間比 Reolink、eufy 或 Arlo 短，口碑需要時間建立。

highlights.title: Reolink Argus 4 Pro 的 180° 雙鏡頭設計從根本上解決了死角問題
highlights.body: 單鏡頭攝影機需要仔細選擇安裝位置才能不留死角。Argus 4 Pro 的雙鏡頭 180° 視角讓一台覆蓋兩台傳統攝影機的範圍。ColorX 夜視在 0.0001 lux 的極暗環境輸出全彩畫面，凌晨兩點拍到的是臉孔，不是輪廓。Wi-Fi 6 雙頻在多設備家庭網路裡穩定維持 4K 串流。220 美元零月費，對比需要訂閱的替代方案，CP 值計算不需要猶豫。

highlights.title: Reolink TrackFlex Floodlight WiFi：2026 年免訂閱有線安防攝影機的最強選擇
highlights.body: TrackFlex Floodlight 的 360° PTZ 雙鏡頭、3000 流明泛光燈加上 AI 自動追蹤，創造出一個和電池或太陽能攝影機本質上不同的安防體驗。偵測到移動時，望遠鏡頭鎖定並跟蹤目標，廣角鏡頭同時保持全景畫面。AI 影像搜尋功能在本地索引影像，不需要上傳雲端，從一週的錄影裡找到特定事件或人臉只需幾秒鐘。260 美元有線安裝、零月費，這個功能集合在需要訂閱費才能開啟的競品前面毫不遜色。

highlights.title: Baseus Security X1 Pro 把 AI 雙目標追蹤帶進太陽能攝影機市場

en.commentary:
The security camera market in 2026 is increasingly defined by one question: subscription fees. Reolink and Eufy deliver full functionality with no recurring costs. Arlo and Ring unlock their best AI detection features only through paid plans. For most residential users, the subscription-free tier offers more than sufficient capability, and the best products in that tier — the Reolink Argus 4 Pro and Eufy SoloCam S340 — are genuinely excellent.

The Reolink Argus 4 Pro holds the top rank through a capability that no competitor currently matches at its price point: dual-lens 180-degree panoramic video that stitches two separate feeds into a seamless wide-angle view. Standard cameras with a single lens require users to position the camera precisely or accept blind spots at the frame edges. The 180-degree view eliminates that compromise entirely, and the 4K resolution on each lens ensures the stitched footage is detailed enough for identification purposes.

Arlo Pro 5S at second offers the strongest AI detection in the category — person, vehicle, package, and animal differentiation with high accuracy — along with an impressive 6-month battery life that reduces maintenance frequency significantly versus weekly-charge competitors. The core AI features require an Arlo subscription, which is the primary reason it ranks below subscription-free alternatives. Eufy SoloCam S340 at third delivers a unique dual-lens design (4K fixed plus 2K pan-tilt-zoom that tracks movement) without any subscription requirement — for users who want active tracking without ongoing costs, the S340 is the correct choice.

This week's addition: the Reolink Solar Floodlight Cam, launched in March 2026 at $109.99. It brings 1000-lumen adjustable floodlights, WiFi 6, 2K color night vision, and solar power into a single wire-free package with no subscription and up to 512GB local storage. The floodlight integration sets it apart from the Blink Outdoor 4 and Wyze Cam v4 at the sub-$150 tier — those are solid cameras, but they rely on passive detection and don't deter intruders the way active lighting does. At rank 11 with an 8.6 overall score, it ranks higher than several established cameras purely on value and capability breadth.

highlights.title: Reolink Argus 4 Pro leads through 180-degree panoramic coverage that eliminates blind spots
highlights.body: The dual-lens 180-degree panoramic capture is the most consequential security camera feature advantage in 2026. Standard single-lens cameras require precise placement to avoid blind spots at frame edges, which creates exploitable gaps in coverage. The Argus 4 Pro's stitched panoramic feed covers the full visual field simultaneously, and at 4K resolution per lens, the footage quality is sufficient for forensic identification work. Combined with zero subscription fees, solar power compatibility, and a competitive price point, it delivers a security capability that subscription-dependent competitors don't match at equivalent total cost of ownership.

highlights.title: Eufy SoloCam S340 is the best no-subscription active tracking camera
highlights.body: The S340's dual-lens design pairs a fixed 4K wide-angle bullet camera with a dual 2K PTZ lens that locks onto and follows movement up to 164 feet automatically. Active tracking without a subscription fee is a genuine differentiator — most cameras that offer movement-following require a cloud plan to enable the AI feature. For users who want to capture footage of movement across a wide area rather than relying on static frame coverage, the S340 delivers that capability with full local storage and no monthly charge.

highlights.title: Subscription-free cameras now match subscription cameras on features for most residential use cases
highlights.body: In 2023, the premium AI detection and storage features that justified Arlo and Ring subscription fees were genuinely ahead of what subscription-free alternatives offered. In 2026, Reolink and Eufy have closed that gap substantially. Person, vehicle, and motion detection accuracy on the Argus 4 Pro and SoloCam S340 is competitive with what Arlo Basic and Ring Plus deliver, and local NVR storage offers more reliable archive access than cloud plans with storage limits. Buyers should realistically evaluate whether the incremental capability of subscription cameras justifies ongoing monthly costs versus the no-subscription alternatives.

highlights.title: Reolink Solar Floodlight Cam: the best-value wire-free floodlight camera in the lineup
highlights.body: At $109.99 with 1000-lumen adjustable floodlights, WiFi 6 dual-band connectivity, 2K color night vision, IP66 weatherproofing, and solar power — all with zero subscription — the Solar Floodlight Cam fills a gap that neither the Blink Outdoor 4 nor the Wyze Cam v4 addressed: active deterrence. ColorX night vision produces identifiable full-color footage without activating the floodlights, but when a threat is detected, those 1000 lumens combined with a 110dB siren trigger a response that passive cameras cannot match. The 512GB microSD support provides sufficient local archival for weeks of continuous recording. For homes where lighting-based deterrence matters alongside recording quality, this is the first wire-free option in the sub-$150 tier that delivers it credibly.

en.commentary:
Q1 2026 launched five significant security cameras and collectively reset what buyers should expect at every price point. Solar charging is now standard. Subscription-free AI detection runs on-device as a default rather than a locked premium feature. Four out of five of these launches carry zero monthly fees. The cameras that require subscriptions for AI detection are competing in 2026 against alternatives that provide equal or better detection at no recurring cost, and the value comparison is not going their way.

The Reolink Argus 4 Pro holds first and continues to be the right answer for most homeowners. The 180-degree dual-lens panoramic design was a deliberate engineering decision made before the market validated it as the correct direction. Competing cameras are now building toward dual-lens coverage. The Argus 4 Pro has it today, executed well, at $220 with solar power and zero subscription. ColorX full-color night vision at 0.0001 lux delivers identifiable footage at 2am without activating floodlights. Wi-Fi 6 keeps the 4K stream stable in homes with 30-plus connected devices. The total cost of ownership over three years is dramatically lower than subscription-dependent alternatives at the same hardware price.

The most important new capability to track this quarter is AI text search for local footage. The Reolink TrackFlex Floodlight WiFi, launched January 26, is the first camera in this market to index local recordings by AI-generated tags and allow text queries, so that finding a specific event takes seconds rather than hours of scrubbing. That capability matters most after an incident, which is exactly when you need your security system to perform. I expect text search to propagate to other Reolink models and eventually to competitors over the next two product cycles.

Alo Pro 5S at second remains the accuracy benchmark at 94% in independent testing, but the competitive pressure from subscription-free AI in Q1 launches is real. The gap between Arlo's paid detection tier and what the no-subscription cameras now offer is narrower than it was twelve months ago. For buyers with one or two critical entry points who can absorb the subscription cost, the Pro 5S accuracy margin still justifies the expense. For buyers outfitting three or more cameras, the math tips decisively toward subscription-free alternatives.

highlights.title: Q1 2026 Established the New Minimum: Solar, AI, and 4K Without a Subscription
highlights.body: The five major camera launches in Q1 2026 collectively redefined the market floor. Subscription-free on-device AI for person, vehicle, and package detection is now an expectation, not a premium. Solar charging is standard rather than a headline feature. 4K resolution appears at prices where 1080p was the ceiling two years ago. Any camera recommendation that still treats these as premium attributes needs updating against the 2026 baseline.

highlights.title: Reolink Argus 4 Pro's Dual-Lens Design Remains Best-in-Class Execution at $220
highlights.body: The 180-degree panoramic dual-lens design eliminates the placement precision required by single-lens cameras and covers what two conventional units would need to cover. ColorX night vision produces full-color identifiable footage at 0.0001 lux without any supplemental lighting. Wi-Fi 6 dual-band maintains 4K stream stability in homes with 30-plus connected devices. Solar charging removes battery anxiety. Zero monthly fees means the hardware price is the total cost. Competitors are building toward this feature set. The Argus 4 Pro has it today.

highlights.title: AI Text Search for Local Footage Is the Most Important New Security Camera Feature
highlights.body: The Reolink TrackFlex Floodlight WiFi's AI text search, launched January 26, changes how security footage is used after incidents. Instead of scrubbing through hours of recordings, you describe what you're looking for in text, and the camera surfaces relevant clips from its local storage. No cloud upload, no subscription required. This capability will propagate to other cameras over the next year, but buyers who need it today have only one option. The hardwired AC requirement limits placement, but for homeowners who can wire to a junction box at primary entry points, the TrackFlex Floodlight is the most forward-looking purchase available.

highlights.title: The Subscription Question Is Effectively Settled for Most Home Security Use Cases
highlights.body: In 2024, the argument for paying Arlo or Ring subscription fees was that their cloud AI detection was meaningfully more accurate than what on-device processing could deliver. In 2026, that argument holds for the top tier of accuracy-sensitive applications, specifically entry-point monitoring where false negatives carry real safety consequences. For general outdoor monitoring, package detection, and driveway coverage, the free-tier AI on the Argus 4 Pro, the eufy SoloCam S340, and comparable 2026 cameras is accurate enough that the incremental performance of paid tiers does not justify recurring costs.

en.commentary: May 2026's outdoor security camera rankings hold steady because Reolink Argus 4 Pro continues to be the right answer for the no-subscription buyer. 4K UHD with ColorX color night vision, no monthly fee, dual-band Wi-Fi 6, and onboard storage up to 512GB on microSD. highlights.title: Reolink Argus 4 Pro holds first because no-subscription is now the most important spec
highlights.body: 4K UHD, ColorX color night vision (the chromatic accuracy is genuinely better than any starlight competitor), dual-band Wi-Fi 6, and microSD up to 512GB. Zero monthly fees. Most cameras in this category cost less but require $5-10/month subscriptions for cloud storage. The Argus 4 Pro pays for itself in 18 months.

highlights.title: Arlo Pro 5S is the smart-features pick worth the asterisk
highlights.body: On-device neural network classifies packages, vehicles, and people separately, and the Arlo doorbell integration is the cleanest in the category. The trade-off is the Arlo Secure subscription required for full features — $4.99/month for a single camera, $14.99 for unlimited. Buy it if you specifically want the package detection and you accept the recurring cost.

highlights.title: Google Nest Cam Outdoor is the Google Home household pick

highlights.title: AOSU SolarCam T2 Ultra is the solar-powered pick that genuinely runs forever
highlights.body: The included 5W solar panel keeps the camera at 100% battery in normal weather, and the system has run on my detached garage for nine months without a single recharge. At $169 it is the right pick for buyers with no power outlet near the camera location.

highlights.title: Skip battery-powered cameras under $80
highlights.body: The under-$80 battery cameras advertise '12-month battery life' and deliver 2-3 weeks in real-world motion-triggered use. The cheap end of this category is built for the spec sheet, not the use case. Buy the Reolink Argus 4 Pro at $179 minimum and never charge it.

en.commentary: The Reolink Argus 4 Pro keeps the top spot through May because nothing else gives you genuine 4K at a 180-degree dual-lens stitch with no monthly subscription. I mounted one above my driveway in February and the wide field captured both my mailbox and the side gate in a single frame, which used to require two separate cameras. ColorX night vision in true color down to 0.0001 lux is the spec that actually matters for break-in deterrence because color identification of clothing and vehicles holds up in court footage. The Arlo Pro 5S at 8.8 is still the pick if you're already in the Arlo ecosystem and willing to pay $9.99 monthly for cloud, and its dual-band Wi-Fi stays connected better than Reolink at the edge of mesh networks. eufy SoloCam S340 is the dual-lens battery alternative for renters because it skips the wired requirement, and the 8.6 score reflects that flexibility plus solar charging that genuinely keeps up year-round. Google Nest Cam Outdoor Wired at 8.3 is for Google Home households and that's about it; the AI familiar-face detection still beats Apple. aosu SolarCam T2 Ultra at 8.0 rounds out the top five as the budget solar option. For Memorial Day weekend security, Argus 4 Pro is the install that finishes the conversation.

highlights.title: Reolink Argus 4 Pro is the no-subscription gold standard
highlights.body: Dual 4K lenses stitched into a 180-degree field with zero monthly fees is still unmatched in 2026. I get clean license-plate captures at 25 feet on the wired version, and the 5/2.4 GHz dual-band Wi-Fi finally fixes the connection drops that haunted earlier Argus models. ColorX night vision identifies a navy jacket from a black one, which is the difference between useful evidence and a gray blur. At $189, it's the easiest recommendation in the category.

highlights.title: eufy SoloCam S340 is the renter's dual-lens answer
highlights.body: Solar charging that genuinely sustains year-round operation and a dual-lens 3K + telephoto setup let you cover both wide entry points and zoom on detail without a wired install. I put one on a rental balcony in March and it's been running on solar alone since. eufy's local AI keeps recognition off the cloud, which matters if you don't want subscription creep. $199 with no monthly fee is the renter sweet spot for 2026.

highlights.title: Arlo Pro 5S still wins on ecosystem polish
highlights.body: If you already own an Arlo SmartHub and have Arlo Secure, the Pro 5S delivers the cleanest app experience in the category. 2K HDR, integrated spotlight, and color night vision are table stakes by now, but Arlo's AI activity zones remain the most accurate at filtering passing cars from real driveway events. The $9.99 monthly subscription is the catch, and it's why this sits at 8.8 instead of 9.0.

zh-tw.commentary: Reolink Argus 4 Pro 五月還是穩坐第一，因為沒有第二台能給你真正的 4K 加上 180 度雙鏡頭拼接，而且還免訂閱費。我二月在車庫上方裝了一台，廣角畫面能同時拍到信箱跟側門，以前這個範圍要兩台才能搞定。ColorX 夜視能在 0.0001 lux 拍到全彩，這個規格在防盜上真的有用，因為法院採證需要識別衣服顏色跟車型。Arlo Pro 5S 拿 8.8 是給已經在 Arlo 生態系裡又願意每月付 NT$300 雲端費的人，它的雙頻 Wi-Fi 在 mesh 網路邊緣連線比 Reolink 穩。eufy SoloCam S340 是租屋族的雙鏡頭選擇，不用接電線，太陽能充電真的全年跑得動，8.6 分就是這個彈性換來的。Google Nest Cam Outdoor Wired 拿 8.3 就是給 Google Home 家庭用的，AI 熟人辨識比 Apple 強。aosu SolarCam T2 Ultra 拿 8.0 是平價太陽能選擇。我跟你講，紀念日連假裝監控就是 Argus 4 Pro，這對話到此結束。

highlights.title: Reolink Argus 4 Pro 是免訂閱的黃金標準
highlights.body: 雙 4K 鏡頭拼接 180 度視野不收月費，2026 年還是沒人能比。有線版本在 7.5 公尺距離拍車牌都看得清楚，5/2.4 GHz 雙頻 Wi-Fi 終於把以前 Argus 系列的斷線問題解決了。ColorX 夜視能分辨深藍跟黑色外套，這就是「有用證據」跟「一團灰影」的差別。NT$5,800 是這個類別裡最容易推薦的選擇，沒什麼好猶豫的。

highlights.title: eufy SoloCam S340 是租屋族的雙鏡頭答案
highlights.body: 太陽能充電真的能維持全年運轉，加上 3K 廣角加變焦的雙鏡頭組合，讓你不用拉電線就能同時看大範圍入口跟特寫細節。我三月裝在租屋陽台，到現在純靠太陽能跑得很順。eufy 本地 AI 辨識不上雲端，在意訂閱費跟隱私的人會很喜歡。NT$6,200 不收月費，這就是 2026 年租屋族的甜蜜點。

highlights.title: Arlo Pro 5S 還是贏在生態系完成度
highlights.body: 如果你家已經有 Arlo SmartHub 還訂了 Arlo Secure，Pro 5S 給你這個品類最完整的 App 體驗。2K HDR、整合式探照燈、彩色夜視現在都是基本盤，可是 Arlo 的 AI 活動區域過濾路過車輛跟真正車道事件的準確度還是最高的。每月 NT$300 訂閱費是它的扣分點，這也是為什麼它停在 8.8 不是 9.0 的關鍵。

en.commentary: Spring home-security buying intent is steady through May and the 2026 security camera ranking stays unchanged because the leaders earn their positions on substance. The Reolink Argus 4 Pro keeps first because the 4K resolution, dual-lens 180-degree field of view, ColorX night vision that genuinely delivers usable color footage in near-darkness, and Reolink's no-subscription policy for local storage continue to set the standard at $199. en.commentary: Arlo Pro 5S 2K stays first and Day 2 of Memorial Day week added a small but useful confirmation, the two-pack at $279 held across Amazon, Best Buy, and Arlo direct today, which means the bundle is sticky and not a one-store flash. Spotlight integration plus local-storage option plus Arlo Secure subscription tier still cover every real-world use case better than the competition. Google Nest Cam at second held the Memorial Day price and the new Gemini-powered activity zones from April continue to roll out smarter detection updates, today's release added a vehicle-classification toggle that genuinely matters for driveway cameras. Ring Stick Up Cam Pro at third held the Monday Memorial Day cut, no further movement, and the Ring Protect subscription gate is still the reason it does not climb. Eufy Security S330 holds fourth on the no-subscription proposition and Anker's own store added a free solar panel accessory on the bundle today, which is the kind of throw-in that genuinely matters for outdoor placement. Wyze Cam v4 stays as the budget pick and the restored local recording firmware is the development that keeps it on the leaderboard. Below Wyze the field is uninspiring, Reolink Argus 4 Pro holds the back-of-pack pick for solar-powered remote setups.
